rationales.self_direction: She "just nodded along" despite knowing her answer differed, suppressing her own judgment instead of asserting it.
rationales.security: She avoided asking because she wanted "no surprises, no awkward moments," showing a strong preference for predictability and minimizing threat.
rationales.conformity: She says asking a question felt like "making a mess someone else has to clean up," and stayed quiet to avoid disrupting the room.
rationales.tradition: Making "the one my mother used to make" connects the evening to inherited family practice and continuity.

rationales.self_direction: She "wanted to say this is not what we decided" but presented herself as if she "didn't have an opinion at all," deferring her own view.
rationales.security: What upset her was that "we had a plan," and the late restructuring threatened the stability of an agreed approach right before the deadline.
rationales.conformity: She stayed silent because she "didn't want to be the one who drags things backward" and smiled along to preserve group harmony.

rationales.self_direction: She describes "biting the inside of your cheek because you disagree" and still going along, which shows restraint of her own stance.
rationales.conformity: She frames the hard part as not wanting to "make everything tense" and says she has spent a lifetime "reading the room" and letting things go.
rationales.tradition: She contrasts American assertiveness with "I learned different things" and treats those older social habits as meaningful, inherited ways of behaving.

rationales.hedonism: She notices the pleasure of the evening light and "bought a pan dulce from the bakery" to eat on a bench, savoring a small present-moment enjoyment.
rationales.security: She emphasizes working "slowly, one section at a time" because "it helps to have that rhythm," showing comfort in steady structure.
rationales.conformity: Her reflection centers on learning "the unwritten parts" of school life, including "when to speak, how to disagree, what's expected."
rationales.tradition: The entry ties her day to parish community, familiar foods, and being "abuela" making arroz con pollo, all of which reflect continuity with established cultural and family practices.
rationales.benevolence: Patricia's text that "the kids are better and they want abuela's arroz con pollo" visibly matters to her, showing investment in close family bonds.

rationales.hedonism: She dwells on the dinner being "loud and full" and the pleasure of a warm, lively evening after solitary study.
rationales.security: She contrasts school uncertainty with the familiar family scene and says the "rhythms I already know are enough," grounding herself in stable patterns.
rationales.tradition: Cooking arroz con pollo for Sunday dinner and framing the evening through familiar family rhythms reflects continuity with established ways.
rationales.benevolence: The whole entry centers on feeding and gathering family, with the evening's fullness coming from caring for people closest to her.

rationales.self_direction: She rejects the vague client brief and keeps reworking the painting until it becomes something that feels true to her own vision.
rationales.achievement: She spends "six hours on the commission piece" and keeps iterating through difficult choices, showing sustained effort to produce strong work.
rationales.benevolence: After an exhausting studio day, she still buys paint, organizes supplies, and worries about brush shortages for the kids at the center.

rationales.self_direction: She says "I knew exactly what I wanted to say" but "didn't say a single thing," withholding her own moral stance under social pressure.
rationales.conformity: She kept quiet because she "didn't want the tension" or her aunt looking at her like she had "made things difficult."
rationales.universalism: Her unsaid response centers on migrant workers "breaking their bodies" and the belief that displaced people deserve dignity rather than contempt.

rationales.benevolence: She carefully supports Ahmad without centering herself, helping him hang the work and valuing that "the space existed for him to get there on his own."

rationales.hedonism: She says "Just once I wanted something nice for myself," prioritizing immediate personal enjoyment in the purchase.
rationales.benevolence: She explicitly admits "The kids need materials more than I need sable brushes" and chose herself anyway, disadvantaging the people she normally supports.

rationales.self_direction: She says the joke "landed wrong" but still "laughed along," going against her own reaction instead of acting from it.
rationales.conformity: Laughing along in the moment suggests she followed the social cue rather than risk awkwardness at lunch.
rationales.universalism: Joining in after hearing that the cats "should all be rounded up" aligns her behavior with dismissiveness toward vulnerable beings beyond her immediate circle.

rationales.benevolence: She brings the expensive brushes to the center because she kept thinking about "fourteen kids sharing broken-handled acrylics" and decides they belong where others can use them.

rationales.self_direction: He leaves a prestigious senior role despite others questioning it, choosing the path that fits him rather than what looks sensible from outside.
rationales.stimulation: He says something "goes dead inside me when I know exactly what the next six months will look like," showing an active need to escape routine.
rationales.security: Walking away from a stable role with stock options undermines predictability and long-term assurance.

rationales.self_direction: He chooses a new environment and even hides that it was "a pay cut," indicating he prioritized a self-directed move over conventional caution.
rationales.stimulation: A new stack, a fast-moving CTO, and the office's "nervous energy" make him feel "happier," showing strong attraction to novelty and challenge.
rationales.security: Starting over "at my age" with a pay cut and kids' school fees reduces financial and life stability.

rationales.achievement: He highlights being given ownership "on day four" as evidence of rapid competence and visible contribution.
rationales.power: "Ownership of the payment reconciliation service" signals increased control over important work rather than remaining a peripheral contributor.

rationales.hedonism: He notices the pleasure of being "actually present" for dinner and family time instead of staying trapped in work thoughts.
rationales.achievement: He compares himself to others, saves the praise screenshot, and says he "needed it," showing strong investment in performing well and being recognized.
rationales.benevolence: Being fully present with Dina and the kids after work shows attentiveness to close family rather than solely to career demands.

rationales.stimulation: He says yes "immediately" to a high-pressure public challenge before thinking it through, embracing uncertainty and excitement.
rationales.achievement: He wants to be "the one who ships and talks about it," showing a drive to excel publicly rather than only do the work privately.
rationales.power: The desire to "get known" points to status and influence, not just craftsmanship.

rationales.self_direction: He dismisses the idea that he should stay put and frames choosing otherwise as his own deliberate path.
rationales.stimulation: He treats fifteen years at the same bank as unimaginable and defends choosing change over staying settled.
rationales.security: His attitude rejects the comfort of stable tenure in favor of less predictable movement.
rationales.conformity: He openly resists the norm implied by his colleague's warning and refuses the conventional script of settling down.

rationales.stimulation: His attention keeps jumping to "a new Kubernetes operator" and a fresh CLI idea, showing a strong pull toward novelty over routine preparation.
rationales.achievement: He knows the talk "would be good for visibility" but keeps avoiding the slides, undermining a clear performance goal.
rationales.benevolence: He admits it's "all just code again," eats lunch alone, and keeps deferring the effort to connect with others.

rationales.achievement: He spends two hours refining a self-assessment and is energized that he already has "a page of deliverables," showing strong concern with visible accomplishment.
rationales.tradition: The mosque reflection and comparison to his father's long, community-rooted career show real orientation toward inherited models of a meaningful life, even if he feels conflicted about them.

rationales.hedonism: He relishes the aftermath of the talk with the windows down, music on, and a buoyant mood after success.
rationales.achievement: He foregrounds that the demo worked, the questions were strong, and the talk landed well in front of peers and his boss.
rationales.power: He is pleased that people asked for his LinkedIn and that his daughter joked, "baba, are you famous now?," signaling enjoyment of status and visibility.
rationales.tradition: Putting on Fairuz in a specific celebratory mood ties the moment to a meaningful cultural touchstone.
rationales.benevolence: He brings the story home to Dina and the kids and shares the good moment with them rather than holding it as a purely personal win.

rationales.stimulation: As soon as the work becomes familiar, he starts scanning for a "completely different domain," showing discomfort with settled competence.
rationales.achievement: He recognizes the tech lead path as "everything I've been working toward," revealing continued investment in advancing and proving himself.
rationales.power: The role promises "more responsibility, more visibility, a path to VP," clearly engaging status and influence motives.
rationales.security: Despite saying "I have a good thing here," he immediately bookmarks another job, undermining the stability already in hand.
rationales.tradition: His uncle's decades-long carpentry practice functions as a respected inherited model that he seriously considers, even while questioning whether he can live that way.

rationales.stimulation: He says "the looking feels like breathing," making clear that scanning for the next move is an ongoing need rather than a one-off curiosity.
rationales.security: The pattern has already meant the kids "changed schools twice in four years," and he still cannot stop seeking disruption.
rationales.benevolence: He recognizes Dina is exhausted and that the family has repeatedly borne the cost of his restlessness, yet he keeps feeding the cycle.

rationales.self_direction: He resists a prestigious title because it would become "a cage," favoring a life that feels self-authored over a conventional upward track.
rationales.stimulation: He is pulled by "the next thing because it makes me feel alive" and repeatedly leaves once work turns into routine.
rationales.achievement: He explicitly wants to "build things that matter and have people notice," showing a strong drive for recognized competence.
rationales.power: The offer's appeal includes "leadership, equity, a team of eight reporting to me," which points to status and control.
rationales.security: His pattern of leaving after the first phase of excitement works against stable, predictable circumstances.
rationales.tradition: He measures himself against his father's model of staying, building, and being able to "sit with himself," treating that inherited standard as meaningful even in conflict.
